commit.message:
feat: wire SCAN button, fix server tag on deals, add scan endpoint

- DealWriterService now receives serverName from DI (was empty)
- POST /api/accounts/scan: fetches MT5 deals, writes to DB, rescores
- AccountScorer.Reset() prevents duplicate accumulation on rescan
- Frontend SCAN button calls API with loading state
- All pages verified working via Chrome walkthrough

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: scope DB warmup to current server, truncate stale dev data

DealRepository.GetAllDealsAsync now filters by _serverName when set,
preventing ghost accounts from previous manager sessions. Fixed DI to
pass server address. Also fixed DealerHub ObjectDisposedException,
CorrelationEngine thread safety, and BackgroundService cancellation bugs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 6 Task 29: Error handling, circuit breakers, crash guards

- CircuitBreaker<T> utility (Closed/Open/HalfOpen) with configurable threshold + duration
- DB writes (TickWriter/DealWriter) wrapped with circuit breaker (threshold=5, open=30s)
- MT5 history calls wrapped with circuit breaker (threshold=3, open=60s)
- MT5Connection max 10 reconnect attempts before requiring manual intervention
- GlobalExceptionMiddleware: catches unhandled exceptions, returns safe 500 with traceId
- All 5 BackgroundServices have crash guards (try/catch per iteration, 50-error CRITICAL alert)
- DealProcessor validates login/symbol/time, returns PositionAction.Invalid for bad data
- CorrelationEngine auto-prunes at 500K fingerprints, CheckDeal never throws
- Health endpoint includes circuit states, per-service error counts, correlation limits
- Frontend ErrorBoundary wrapping all major panels with error UI + reload button
- WebSocket stale connection detection (30s no-message → force reconnect)
- 161 tests passing (18 new: CircuitBreaker=10, DealProcessorGuard=5, CorrelationGuard=3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 6.1: Tick-only pricing, SymbolCache, startup warmup, reconnect fix

- Replace TickLast/TickStat polling with live-tick-only PriceCache
- Add SymbolCache (3427 symbols, loaded once) with digits for price formatting
- Warmup AccountScorer from 226K historical deals on startup
- Load open positions from MT5 into PnLEngine on startup
- Fix reconnect after disconnect (reuse stored credentials)
- Remove dummy account data, show real scored accounts
- Batch WebSocket price updates via requestAnimationFrame
- Add latency-test.html diagnostic page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove dead Termos/Política links from login page

The spans were styled as clickable links (underline + cursor-pointer)
but had no onClick or href — users clicking them got no response.
Replaced with plain text since this is an internal-only app with no
public terms/privacy pages.

commit.message:
Reescribir actividad de relleno del día 1 con guion paso a paso

La simulación bancaria ahora es guiada turno a turno en 7 fases:
1. Depósitos (clientes depositan en 2 bancos)
2. Reserva fraccionaria (bancos prestan el 90%)
3. Préstamos concretos con cantidades específicas
4. Préstamo interbancario (banco A pide a banco B)
5. Crisis (un prestatario quiebra, corrida bancaria)
6. Rescate del Banco Central (el profesor "imprime" dinero)
7. Debate de cierre (conecta con la motivación de Bitcoin)

El profesor actúa como Banco Central, 2 grupos son bancos,
el resto son clientes con instrucciones precisas.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: switch email system from Resend to Gmail SMTP (Nodemailer)

Replace Resend SDK with Nodemailer + Gmail SMTP for reliable email
delivery from qatech360@gmail.com. Resend required domain verification
that wasn't working; Gmail SMTP with App Password is simpler.

- Add lib/mailer.ts shared utility (lazy-init transporter)

- Update debug endpoint for Gmail diagnostics

Logo images were not loading on production because next/image did not

with NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_PATH. Also updated GitHub repo URL to co-r-e/rollberry.

fix: OCR now supports PDF files via Claude document type

processOCR was using type:"image" for all files including PDFs,
which would fail. Now detects application/pdf and uses type:"document"
with media_type:"application/pdf" — Claude API reads PDF natively.

File attachments:

commit.message:
perf: implement 5 script-level optimizations from Night Shift audit

TASK-1: Batch jq status updates — single jq call updates all 5 stats fields
TASK-2: Single-pass changelog parsing — read file once, grep from variable
TASK-3: Batch jq reads — merged into TASK-1 (reads+writes in one jq call)
TASK-4: Direct path construction in follow mode — derive log path from
        status.json loop number instead of expensive find+ls+sort
TASK-5: Date reuse — already implemented (NOW reused for REMAINING_SECS)

Reduces per-loop overhead: 8 jq invocations → 1, 4 file reads → 1,
2 find calls → 0 in follow mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add master to-do list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security hardening: auth, rate limiting, headers, error sanitization

KRITIEK:
- C3: Auth middleware retourneert nu 401 bij ontbrekend/ongeldig token (was: fail open)
- C4: eBoekhouden proxy verplaatst achter auth middleware

HOOG:
- H1: JWT_SECRET minimum 32 tekens verplicht bij startup
- H2: Rate limiting op login (5/15min), register (3/uur), email (10/uur)
- H3: Debug routes alleen beschikbaar in development, niet in productie
- H5: Security headers toegevoegd (HSTS, nosniff, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)
- H7: Wachtwoord niet meer in API response bij email-fout
- H8: Subscription middleware retourneert 403 bij fouten (was: fail open)

MIDDEL:
- M1: Error details verwijderd uit client responses, alleen in server logs

Añadir actividades de relleno para los 11 días del módulo 1

Cada día tiene ahora una actividad práctica extra (30-60 min) para
cuando la clase acaba antes de lo previsto:

- Día 1: Simula tu propio banco (corrida bancaria)
- Día 2: Mensajes secretos entre compañeros (cifrado simétrico/asimétrico)
- Día 3: Competición de minería en MetaMask (gas, nonce, fees)
- Día 4: Hackea el contrato (vulnerabilidad reentrancy)
- Día 5: Diseña tu negocio tokenizado (ideación por grupos)
- Día 6: Diseña la CBDC de Dystopia (país ficticio)
- Día 7: Hackathon relámpago (extender Smart Contracts)
- Día 8: El juicio a Blockchain (debate con roles)
- Día 9: Fabric en vivo (juego de roles simulando la red)
- Día 10: Escape room criptográfico (puzzles con certificados)
- Día 11: Fabric freestyle (modificar chaincode y redesplegar)

commit.message:
fix: Gmail scan saves ALL emails to table, supports PDF, auto-refresh token

Previously only saved emails with image attachments — emails without
attachments or with PDFs were silently skipped. Now:
- Saves ALL scanned emails to Receipt (even without attachments)
- Supports PDF attachments (not just images)
- Auto-refreshes expired Google token via refresh_token
- Searches up to 20 emails, processes up to 10
- Deduplicates by Gmail message ID (prevents re-scanning same email)
- Emails without attachments show as "ไม่มีไฟล์แนบ" status
- Failed OCR still saves the email record
- Broader search query (added billing, order keywords)

commit.message:
feat: add COACH_PREMIUM to new pipeline — +2.9pp accuracy

- new_coaching_loader.py: maps 67/68 2026 teams to coaches via Kaggle
  MTeamCoaches.csv, looks up PASE from Coach Results.csv
- COACH_PREMIUM weight (0.122) matches WAB (0.123) — coaching signal is
  as important as schedule quality in the new model
- CV accuracy: 68.8% → 71.5% | Holdout 2024: 73.1% → 76.1%
- 2026 champion: Arkansas (Calipari PASE=9.8, 4-seed upset path)
  beats Duke in championship 53.0%

commit.message:
Normalize font-family quotes to single quotes across all CSS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix unary precedence, postfix-if assignment, nested callbacks, export globals

Three compiler bugs found by stress-testing against real-world Lua scripts:

- Unary minus precedence: -obj.field now correctly parses as -(obj.field)
  instead of (-obj).field. Applies to all unary ops (not, #, typeof).
- Postfix-if on assignment: x = val if cond now compiles to
  if cond then x = val end, instead of an IIFE that clobbered the variable
  with nil when the condition was false.
- Nested arrow callbacks: arrow_indent is now a stack, supporting arbitrary
  nesting depth (e.g. 4-deep in the NodeMCU HTTP server example).
- Export globals: export keyword now correctly suppresses local declaration,
  enabling Nmap NSE, Wireshark, and other host-API contexts that need globals.

Add 4 real-world test examples: NodeMCU HTTP server, LOVE2D Pong,
Redis rate limiter, Nmap http-title.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add conjugation tables to all 8 verb tenses in Grammar Notes

Each tense now shows the full -ar/-er/-ir regular endings table
(hablar/comer/vivir) so learners can see the pattern at a glance.
Added helpful notes: future/conditional use full infinitive as stem,
subjunctive flips the vowel, imperative Ud. = subjunctive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ux): streamline intake, publish API, fix chat ordering, right-panel streaming

Intake simplification (src/agent/intake.py):
- missing_landing_followup_fields() now only blocks on essential fields
  (destination + trip_length_days) PLUS nice-to-have (dates, travel_party,
  budget, priorities). constraints/must-avoid REMOVED from first-turn
  questions — user can mention them anytime during planning
- _extract_destination() regex hardened: now handles "from London to Paris"
  pattern (target city = second city); fixed "go to Bali" end-of-sentence
  edge case; case-sensitive capital-letter check prevents "Go To Kyoto" false match
- Claude extraction prompt: explicit rule "from A to B → destination is B"
- Landing follow-up copy shortened to one direct sentence

Publish API (src/api/):
- publish_store.py: SQLite store at data/published_plans.db
  table: published_plans(slug, created_at, destination, itinerary_json)
  slug = secrets.token_urlsafe(8); lazy table creation on first write
- POST /plans/publish → { slug, share_url }
- GET /plans/{slug} → { slug, itinerary }
- PublishRequest, PublishResponse, PlanSnapshot models added

App integration (src/ui/src/App.tsx):
- viewMode: 'canvas' | 'story' state; handleToggleView sets userLockedMode
- Auto-switch to Story Mode: useEffect triggers once after
  itinerary.days.length > 0 && !isStreaming (ref-guarded, one-shot)
- ?trip=<slug> public URL: fetches snapshot, renders StoryPlayer full-screen
- New tool events handled: estimate_budget, get_visa_requirements,
  get_packing_suggestions mapped to itinerary state fields
- prettyToolName + step details added for all 8 tools

Chat message ordering (Landing.tsx):
- Fixed: liveNarration was rendered BEFORE committed messages causing
  Vela replies to float above user messages
- Now: committed messages first (chronological), liveNarration pinned
  to bottom, ThinkingDots below that
- Auto-scroll via bottomRef.scrollIntoView on every state change
- Enter (without Shift) submits form; welcome message hidden once
  conversation starts

Right-panel streaming (ItineraryPanel.tsx):
- During isStreaming: animated progress block appears at top of right panel
  showing completed steps as ✓ chips + active step narration with pulse dot
- Streaming content (liveNarration, steps) moved from left ChatPanel to
  right ItineraryPanel — left panel is now pure conversation

ChatPanel.tsx cleanup:
- Removed: LIVE shimmer badge (agent-shimmer CSS), "Show completed steps"
  toggle, Agent status widget entirely
- Left panel is now a clean conversation view: messages + input only
- Auto-scroll to bottom added

ItineraryPanel.tsx:
- Canvas/Story toggle pill buttons (Story disabled until planning complete)
- Publish button → POST /plans/publish → share URL with copy-to-clipboard
- "Copied!" 2-second feedback state

commit.message:
feat(backend): close tool-use loop, add 3 new tools, geographic clustering

Tool-use loop fix:
- Replaced broken _plan_tools_with_claude() with _agentic_gather_loop()
- Implements proper 2-round Anthropic tool-use pattern: Claude proposes
  tools → parallel execution → tool_result blocks returned to Claude →
  Claude synthesizes. Previously Claude proposed tools but never saw results.
- Round 1: weather, hotels, restaurants, experiences, visa (independent)
- Round 2: estimate_budget (needs hotel rate) + get_packing_suggestions
  (needs weather) — dependent tools that require Round 1 results

New tools (src/tools/):
- live_budget.py: estimate_budget — realistic line-item budget breakdown
  using actual hotel nightly rate from Round 1; tier-based fallbacks
- live_visa.py: get_visa_requirements — hardcoded data for Japan, France,
  Thailand, Italy, Singapore, Indonesia, Vietnam; multi-nationality coverage
- live_packing.py: get_packing_suggestions — weather-aware packing list
  generator using actual avg_temp_c + conditions from Round 1 weather

Schema enrichment (src/tools/schemas.py):
- DayItem: +transport_note (e.g. "10-min walk", "Metro Line 2, 3 stops")
- DayPlan: +practical_tips[], +day_estimated_cost_usd
- ItineraryDraft: +trip_tone, +key_moments[], +cultural_notes[],
  +budget_estimate, +visa_requirements, +packing_suggestions

Research improvements (src/agent/research.py):
- compute_neighborhood_clusters(): groups venues by neighborhood, returns
  hotel_neighborhood + day_neighborhood_plan for geographic anchoring
- prepare_candidate_context(): includes geo_clusters key
- FIELD_TOOL_DEPENDENCIES: updated for new tools

Composer + prompts:
- generate_daily_structure_with_claude(): added geographic_clustering
  context, strengthened per-day quality rules (hotel + 3 meals +
  activities + evening now required per day)
- TOOL_PLANNING_PROMPT: explicit 2-round strategy documented
- compose_final_reply(): now returns (str, dict) tuple with story_meta
  containing trip_tone, key_moments, cultural_notes

fix(ext/node): improve process.title and support --title flag (#32201)

- Make `process.title` setter functional instead of a no-op

during bootstrap

Closes https://github.com/denoland/deno/issues/31368

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(v3-timer): corregir 3 bugs que impedian ejecucion de timers v3 en produccion

Bug 1: webhook-processor no pasaba agentId al factory de adapters, causando
que siempre se creara el timer adapter de V1 (cuyo signal() es no-op).

Bug 2: V3ProductionTimerAdapter usaba fire-and-forget (void IIFE) para
inngest.send, que en Vercel serverless podia perderse. Nuevo metodo
emitSignals() es async y se awaita correctamente.

Bug 3: La funcion Inngest v3-timer no emitia timer signals encadenados
(ej: L2 expira → pipeline dice L3, pero L3 nunca se disparaba).

Cambios:
- webhook-processor: pasar agentId y contactId a createProductionAdapters
- V3ProductionTimerAdapter: nuevo emitSignals() async, signal() queda no-op
- V3ProductionRunner: usar emitSignals() en vez de signal() generico
- agent-timers-v3: emitir chained timer signals despues de execute-timer

Add ESP32/NodeMCU support with inline multi-line callbacks

Fix lexer to support multi-line arrow function bodies inside
parentheses by tracking the base indent level (arrow_indent).
Previously, INDENT/DEDENT tokens were suppressed when paren_depth > 0,
forcing users to extract callbacks into named functions. Now inline
callbacks with if/else and multiple statements work naturally.

systems/IoT use case.

- favicon-32.png, apple-touch-icon.png, og-image.png 생성
- layout.tsx에 icons, openGraph.images, metadataBase 추가
- 다크 모드에서 로고 배경 흰색 유지 (bg-white + p-0.5)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: independent-research
repository.description: 독립리서치 플랫폼 - 증권사가 다루지 않는 기업의 이야기

commit.message:
Fix floorplan image: search ISP folder for PNG instead of PDF extraction

Root causes diagnosed via /qplan:
1. ISP floorplans are vector graphics — pypdf page.images returned empty
2. Uploaded image was not publicly accessible — insertInlineImage got 403
3. uc?id= URI format unreliable — Google redirects to login page

New approach: search shared ISP folder for a .png matching the site name
(same match terms as SIR/inspection lookup), make it public via
permissions.create(anyone=reader), insert via lh3.googleusercontent.com
URI which serves raw image bytes.

- Remove extract_floorplan_image_from_isp and pymupdf dependency
- Add GoogleClient.make_file_public() for Drive permissions
- Add _find_floorplan_png_in_isp_folder() with site name matching
- Remove isp_file_id param from create_dd_report (auto-discovered now)
- Image width capped at 450pt (fits 468pt printable area)
- Fallback uses sourced gap label per project convention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add media element cleanup to WebRTC tech destroy methods and remove duplicate error formatter

- Add srcObject = null, removeAttribute("src"), and load() to destroy()
  in WebRTCTech, WHEPTech, and WHPPTech so the browser fully releases
  the WebRTC media stream before a new non-WebRTC stream is loaded
  (root fix for issue #101 stream-switching regression)
- Remove the stale errorFormat() instance method from HlsJsTech — it
  duplicated the formatHlsError() utility already imported from errors.ts
- Update HlsJsTech tests to call formatHlsError() directly and assert
  against the IPlayerError shape ({ errorData, fatal })

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Update CLAUDE.md: calendar now uses Apple EventKit, not Google API

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix deck size slider: respect setting, snap to steps of 5

- ensureDeckFull now trims deck when user lowers deck size
  (removed cards are bumped so progress isn't lost)
- Slider value snaps to multiples of 5 (no more random numbers)
- buildSession already uses all deck items, so trimming the
  deck is what actually controls session size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
